key_insight: The paper completes the symmetry classification of nonrelativistic odd-parity spin splitting in collinear magnets, showing allowed classes from p- and f-wave up to h-, k-, and m-wave with m-wave as the upper bound.
engineering_relevance: This provides a design rule for creating and selecting magnetic materials or light-driven phases with targeted spin splitting and potentially controllable anomalous Hall responses.
method: Used group theory to map crystallographic point-group irreducible representations to lowest-order odd-parity basis functions.
Derived a coupling rule linking symmetry-breaking axial fields and parent Néel order to the induced spin-splitting class.
Built minimal lattice models and screened MAGNDATA to identify real materials and light-induced phase control cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a database screening plus symmetry analysis and Berry-curvature calculations, including light-driven h-wave and k-wave phases in Fe2TeO6 and MgFe6Ge6 and corresponding anomalous Hall predictions.
watch_item: The classification is symmetry-based, so realizing the predicted high-partial-wave phases still depends on achieving the required magnetic order or external-field conditions in practice.
paper_title: Complete Hierarchy of Nonrelativistic Odd-Parity Spin Splitting in Collinear Magnets
